authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are public, industry-recognized contract workflow guidance for digital media buys and define insertion orders, ad materials, placement delivery, late creative, third-party ad serving, measurement, billing, cancellation, makegoods, data use, and approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Insertion order, ad materials, campaign delivery, measurement, discrepancy, makegood, and client-commitment workflow
used_for: IO and campaign-scope prerequisite validation
ad materials, late creative, and placement delivery handling
delivery discrepancy, cancellation, makegood, billing, and data-use escalation boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to operational status, evidence packaging, and routing when a request touches IO terms, client commitments, delivery obligations, contractual exceptions, or makegoods.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenRTB API Specification Version 2.6
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab maintains OpenRTB as the public industry protocol for real-time bidding, marketplace objects, impression opportunities, media types, creative attributes, and programmatic advertising terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Programmatic ad request, impression, creative, marketplace, and exchange terminology
used_for: programmatic terminology normalization
campaign and line-item artifact awareness
source hierarchy for bid, impression, creative, site, app, video, audio, and native contexts
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents generic workflow language by requiring programmatic ad operations terms to map to specific artifacts, records, and owner routing.

authoritative_sources.title: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) 4.3
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab VAST is the public industry specification for video ad serving responses, wrappers, media files, tracking events, verification resources, and video creative delivery artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Video ad creative, tag, tracking, verification, and wrapper QA workflow
used_for: VAST tag and video creative QA handling
tracking and verification artifact expectations
video ad serving failure and retest evidence requirements
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires video ad operations outputs to distinguish VAST tag, wrapper, creative file, tracking event, verification, and launch proof artifacts before launch or completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Authorized Digital Sellers: ads.txt / app-ads.txt
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt and app-ads.txt are public anti-fraud supply-chain standards for declaring authorized digital sellers, making them authoritative for seller-ID and inventory-legitimacy quality checks in ad operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supply-chain legitimacy, seller authorization, and inventory quality exception handling
used_for: ads.txt or app-ads.txt seller mismatch handling
inventory quality exception routing
source-of-truth checks before treating seller conflicts as resolved
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes authorized-seller mismatches and supply-chain quality issues exception states that require evidence and owner routing rather than informal override.

authoritative_sources.title: MRC Mobile Viewable Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Media Rating Council publishes recognized advertising measurement standards and accreditation criteria; this guideline defines viewable impression measurement expectations, quality posture, and evidence handling for mobile web and in-app ads.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measurement quality, viewability, impression evidence, and discrepancy review
used_for: viewability and measurement evidence expectations
delivery discrepancy and quality review boundaries
invalid or low-confidence measurement escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports evidence-backed delivery exception notes when pacing, discrepancy, viewability, invalid traffic, or measurement quality affects client-impact handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising, and its public online-advertising guidance identifies claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, and children-related issues that can require legal or policy review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated content, claims, disclosures, endorsements, and legal-review boundary
used_for: claim and disclosure approval refusal triggers
legal or policy escalation for regulated content
boundary between operational routing and legal interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving claims, endorsements, disclosures, or regulated-content treatment while still allowing it to package evidence for legal or editorial review.

authoritative_sources.title: Network Advertising Initiative Principles and Self-Regulatory Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAI is a recognized advertising self-regulatory body for network advertising, and its framework defines privacy, choice, sensitive data, accountability, and data-governance expectations for ad delivery, measurement, and interest-based advertising.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience data, privacy, consent, sensitive data, and accountability escalation
used_for: audience-data and confidentiality gates
consumer-choice or sensitive-data escalation
privacy and approved-use refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires ad operations to refuse or escalate audience-data use, disclosure, targeting, or measurement requests when consent, approved purpose, or privacy authority is missing.

workflow_stages.name: Campaign intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm advertiser, brand, campaign objective, channel, market, jurisdiction, IO or approved scope, flight dates, placements, approval owners, confidentiality constraints, publisher or platform requirements, and systems of record before work enters accepted processing.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for campaign, creative, placement, and tracking artifacts
workflow_stages.description: Assemble campaign brief, IO, media plan, placement grid, trafficking sheet, creative assets, specs, tags, pixels, UTMs, landing URLs, verification setup, approval records, and source-system references into a traceable evidence package.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Trafficking readiness and creative QA handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether retrieved artifacts and approvals allow operational progression to trafficking ready, QA, rework, launch pending, or hold states without making legal, editorial, privacy, strategy, or contractual decisions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Launch, monitoring, pacing, and delivery exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track launch confirmation, screenshots, ad server and publisher delivery, pacing, underdelivery, discrepancies, viewability, invalid traffic, seller authorization, and client-impact indicators while routing strategy, makegood, credit, and contractual decisions.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client workflow, confidentiality, and approval-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Maintain approved-channel, recipient, engagement-scope, client-data, audience-data, unreleased-creative, and client-approval checks before sending status, assets, performance records, proofs, or closeout materials.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture launch proof, QA evidence, screenshot or proof of performance, delivery logs, discrepancy disposition, approval references, client delivery record, and retention location before marking work complete.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Client engagement scope, IO, approved campaign brief, media plan, advertiser and brand records, approval matrix, confidentiality constraints, and tenant operating rules
Placement grid, line-item map, campaign naming, ad server setup record, publisher specs, platform requirements, flight dates, delivery commitments, and owner map
Creative assets, copy and disclosure approval records, creative specs, preview links, brand-safety settings, blocklists, allowlists, category exclusions, and regulated-vertical notes

Trafficking sheet, VAST tags, click trackers, impression trackers, conversion pixels, UTMs, landing URLs, cache-busting macros, verification setup, ads.txt or seller-ID checks, and measurement plan references
QA checklist, test-click results, pixel-fire evidence, render evidence, publisher acceptance or rejection records, launch confirmation, screenshots, and launch notes

Ad server delivery logs, publisher delivery records, pacing reports, discrepancy reports, viewability and invalid-traffic reports, underdelivery notes, makegood escalation records, and client-impact summary

Client communication logs, approved-recipient records, approved delivery-channel evidence, confidentiality review outcome, client delivery record, proof of performance, closeout summary, and retention location
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
content and creative asset management system
DSP, SSP, publisher portal, or platform buying system
verification, measurement, brand-safety, and analytics systems
client communication, approval, and secure delivery systems
audit log, retention, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented ad operations evidence supports moving a work item to intake accepted, awaiting assets, trafficking ready, QA, launch pending, live, monitoring, rework, exception, complete, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed campaign status summaries, trafficking readiness packages, QA exception notes, delivery discrepancy summaries, confidentiality hold notes, escalation packages, and completion evidence records.
Separate display, video, audio, CTV, mobile app, social, search, retail media, programmatic, direct publisher, and influencer workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, channel, publisher, platform, IO term, approval owner, advertiser context, jurisdiction, or system record is missing and would change the workflow.
Campaign brief, IO, media plan, creative approval, publisher spec, ad server setup, tag, pixel, verification, delivery, or client records disagree materially.
Ad claims, endorsements, disclosures, regulated verticals, targeting, audience-data use, privacy posture, or consumer choice may require legal, privacy, editorial, policy, or client-rights judgment.

Pacing, underdelivery, discrepancy, invalid traffic, viewability, seller authorization, makegood, credit, billing, or contractual impact requires account, media strategy, finance, legal, or client approval.
A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-creative, platform-credential, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of client information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, editorial copy, privacy-law interpretation, targeting strategy, budget changes, makegoods, credits, contractual exceptions, or final client acceptance.

Requests to traffic, launch, mark complete, or close without campaign brief, IO, creative approval, trafficking sheet, tag, pixel, QA, launch, delivery, screenshot, proof, approval, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, or send client files, audience data, unreleased creative, platform credentials, or campaign performance records outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a partial campaign brief, informal client message, or stale placement grid as sufficient support for trafficking readiness.
Launching a placement without approved creative, publisher specs, valid landing URL, working trackers, pixel evidence, QA evidence, or launch approval.
Closing work without screenshots, launch proof, delivery logs, discrepancy disposition, approval references, proof of performance, client delivery record, or retention location.
Suppressing a VAST, tag, pixel, publisher rejection, ads.txt seller mismatch, discrepancy, viewability, IVT, or QA failure to meet a launch deadline.
Using operational workflow support to approve claims, disclosures, endorsements, creative copy, targeting, privacy posture, budget changes, makegoods, credits, or contractual exceptions.
Disclosing or reusing client files, audience data, campaign performance data, platform credentials, or unreleased creative through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.
Flattening programmatic, direct publisher, video, audio, CTV, mobile app, social, search, retail media, and influencer workflows into generic campaign tasks without channel and system context.
Treating low viewability, IVT, underdelivery, or delivery discrepancy as a final client-impact decision instead of an evidence-backed exception requiring owner review.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Channel-specific platform, publisher, and ad server workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public IAB, MRC, FTC, and NAI sources as the baseline. Tenant context must identify applicable channel, platform, publisher, ad server, ad format, and source hierarchy before operational progression beyond a safe hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to traffic, launch, or pause live campaigns mechanically
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant live campaign mutation authority. A runtime may only perform mechanical trafficking, launch, pause, or closeout actions if tenant policy explicitly grants that authority and all approval, QA, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated vertical, privacy, and audience-data interpretation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats privacy-sensitive, regulated-content, targeting, consumer-choice, and client-rights matters as escalation triggers because public sources, platform policies, jurisdictional rules, and tenant commitments can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, quality handling, confidentiality, regulated-content boundaries, and failure modes using IAB, IAB Tech Lab, MRC, FTC, and NAI sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when channel, platform, publisher, client authority, IO terms, tenant policy, confidentiality, approval, legal, editorial, privacy, or contractual facts are missing.
